commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=036dc10616593bfe8a1855631503a5a60288e0c1)
commands.review_summary:
Raises the shared compaction `SAFETY_MARGIN` from 1.2 to 1.5, adds tests asserting the new chunk-budget behavior, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is established. Source inspection confirms the dependency uses chars/4 and OpenClaw already documents a 2-4x CJK undercount, but the PR has not shown a real CJK compaction overflow or after-fix run.
commands.review_followup: Contributor iteration or maintainer judgment is needed because the automated lane cannot supply the contributor's required real CJK compaction proof and the solution shape should change before merge.

target.title: fix(compaction): increase token estimation safety margin to 50%
target.body:
## Problem

The `SAFETY_MARGIN` constant in `src/agents/compaction.ts` is set to 1.2 (20% buffer) to compensate for `estimateTokens()` inaccuracy. The code comments at line 247 explicitly acknowledge the limitation: *"chars/4 heuristic misses multi-byte chars, special tokens, code tokens, etc."*

A 20% buffer is insufficient for:
- **CJK text**: characters average ~1 token each vs ~4 chars/token for ASCII, so the heuristic underestimates by ~4x
- **Code with operators/braces**: each symbol consumes a full token
- **Base64/encoded content**: dense bytes inflate token count disproportionately

This causes the compaction chunking and threshold calculations to underestimate token usage, leading to context overflow.

## Fix

Increase `SAFETY_MARGIN` from 1.2 to 1.5 (50% buffer). This is the simplest conservative fix that helps for all content types without requiring a CJK-detection heuristic. The `estimateTokens()` function is imported from `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` and not controlled in this repo, so the margin is the downstream mitigation.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: token estimation safety margin increased from 20% to 50% to reduce context overflow from content types where the chars/4 heuristic significantly underestimates.
Real environment tested: local source checkout of openclaw/openclaw at commit 71b79f008d (current upstream/main) on Linux x86_64, Node v26.0.0, vitest 4.1.6.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm tsgo:core (type check); node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts (compaction test suite).
Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

Type check:
```
$ pnpm tsgo:core
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
```
No errors.

Compaction test suite (178 tests across 2 project scopes):
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  178 passed (178)
```

Observed result after fix: all 178 compaction tests pass with the increased margin. Type check is clean. The change is a single constant value update.
What was not tested: actual CJK conversation compaction (would require a running OpenClaw instance with CJK-heavy conversations hitting the compaction threshold). A content-adaptive margin (detecting CJK ratio and adjusting dynamically) would be a more precise fix but is a larger design change.
